assertionResults.reason: gemini-extension.json parsed as valid JSON and the real Gemini CLI accepted the extension link command from the project path with exit code 0.
evidence: jq . /Users/codex/Projects/gemini-mission-control-extension/gemini-extension.json exited 0 and printed the expected required fields: name, version, description, contextFileName, commandsDir, skillsDir, hooksDir.
gemini --version returned 0.33.0, confirming the real CLI surface was available.
gemini extensions link /Users/codex/Projects/gemini-mission-control-extension exited with code 0 and printed installation/linking messages for extension 'gemini-mission-control'.

assertionResults.reason: The manifest-referenced GEMINI.md file exists, is 241 lines long, and contains all required user-visible sections defining the orchestrator contract.
evidence: Section headings found: ## 1. Project Description, ## 2. Orchestrator Identity, ## 3. State Management Protocol, ## 4. Available Commands, ## 5. Available Agents, ## 7. State Machine.

Manual/targeted inspection confirmed state management file-tool guidance (read_file/write_file/replace), command listing, agent listing, and valid state names planning/orchestrator_turn/worker_running/handoff_review/paused/completed/failed.

assertionResults.reason: All manifest path references resolve within the extension directory without broken links.
evidence: Manifest values observed: contextFileName=GEMINI.md, commandsDir=commands, skillsDir=skills, hooksDir=hooks.
test -d /Users/codex/Projects/gemini-mission-control-extension/commands passed and LS showed the command files.
test -d /Users/codex/Projects/gemini-mission-control-extension/hooks passed and LS showed hooks.json.

frictions.description: README.md was absent at the repo root, so initial codebase exploration had to fall back to GEMINI.md plus mission/testing guidance.
frictions.resolution: Used GEMINI.md, mission AGENTS.md, and user-testing.md as the primary context sources.

frictions.description: gemini extensions link displayed an interactive trust/continue prompt even though it completed with exit code 0 in this environment.
frictions.resolution: Recorded the real CLI output and relied on the successful exit code plus install/link messages as user-visible evidence.

summary: Tested 3 assigned foundation assertions through the real Gemini CLI and direct filesystem checks. VAL-FOUND-001, VAL-FOUND-002, and VAL-FOUND-013 all passed; manifest JSON is valid, extension linking succeeded with exit code 0, GEMINI.md contains the required orchestrator sections, and every manifest-referenced path resolves correctly.
